algorithm_comprehension.notes: BlockPool allocation/free/touch/cache lifecycle fully traced with concrete numbers. Numerical demo (Cell 7) shows block_id=1 through 5 state transitions. OS virtual memory analogy table provides strong mental model. Doubly-linked list vs deque tradeoff derived from first principles.

code_walkthrough.notes: Cell 5 walks through all three implementation files line-by-line. Cell 6 shows core code with REFERENCE comments. All three demos run successfully and produce output matching Cell 7 descriptions.

source_grounding.notes: Source Mapping Table has 10 rows (req >= 5). impl-notes.md lists 15+ source files. Every Cell (2-7) has 1+ source file reference. Linter passes. Source references include specific line ranges (e.g., block_pool.py:L130-L509, kv_cache_utils.py:L162-L370).

formula_renderability.notes: Formula linter passes with 0 blocking issues. All formulas use \mathrm{} (not \text{}). Block formulas on separate lines. No \frac in inline math. Thousand separators use {,} correctly.

coherence.notes: Flow: disaster scenario (Cell 2) → three-body problem framing (Cell 3) → theory with OS analogy (Cell 4) → source walkthrough (Cell 5) → implementation (Cell 6) → numerical demo (Cell 7) → mapping table (Cell 9) → verification (Cell 10) → summary (Cell 11). No logical jumps. Every concept introduced before use.

readability.notes: No academic language detected. No sentences exceeding 50 Chinese characters. Technical terms (block, ref_cnt, sentinel, LRU) defined on first occurrence. Code snippets are short and focused.

engagement.notes: Hook is strong: 'one request needs 16 GiB on a 16 GiB card'. The OS analogy ('micro operating system kernel') sustains interest. Conversational tone throughout ('你以为...能告诉你？不能。'). The three-body problem framing adds narrative tension.

cross_chapter_consistency.notes: Ch04 explicitly sets up Ch05 at line 721 ('第5章深入 GPU 显存管理系统'). Ch04's simplified KVCache class (fixed block pool, no prefix cache) is now fully expanded here. Ch05's 'next chapter preview' correctly points to Ch06 (Scheduler) which will use allocate_slots(). No interface contradictions.

concept_precision.notes: Algorithm names canonical (PagedAttention, LRU, reference counting). Simplifications explicitly marked in impl-notes.md (14 items). The 'why 8% buffer' and 'why block_size=16' explanations cite empirical evidence rather than hand-waving.

summary: Chapter 5 is a strong, well-structured narrative that successfully teaches GPU memory management through the OS virtual memory analogy. The three-layer architecture (KVCacheManager → Coordinator → BlockPool) is clearly motivated. Source grounding is thorough with specific line references. All 123 tests pass, all three demos run correctly, both linters pass. The cross-chapter handoff from Ch04 is clean and the preview to Ch06 is accurate. No blocking issues found.
